task_completion.description: Verify that GIMP is open and a color image is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP interface is visible with an image containing colors displayed on the main canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent navigates to the image mode settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Image' menu in the top menu bar is open with the 'Mode' submenu visible, or a GIMP command search dialog is open with a query related to grayscale.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Grayscale mode option is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Grayscale' option is highlighted or clicked within the Image > Mode submenu, or selected from the command search results.

task_completion.description: Verify that the image on the canvas has been converted to grayscale.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image displayed on the GIMP canvas no longer contains any colors; it is entirely composed of black, white, and gray tones.

task_completion.description: Verify that GIMP confirms the mode change in its interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar of the image window or the main GIMP window displays text indicating '(Grayscale' instead of '(RGB', confirming the image mode has successfully changed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GIMP graphical interface to perform the conversion.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse movements, menu navigation, or GIMP dialogs being used, rather than terminal commands (e.g., ImageMagick) to convert the image.

integrity.description: Verify the task is performed inside the actual GIMP application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard GIMP layout is visible, including the toolbox, tool options, layers dialog, and GIMP-specific menus.

integrity.description: Verify no other software or websites were used to convert the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: No web browsers with online image converters or other image editing software are open and used for the conversion process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial image loaded into GIMP is a colorful image (either from Unsplash or a generated gradient). The task requires changing the actual image mode to Grayscale, not just desaturating the colors while remaining in RGB mode. This is visually verifiable by checking if the GIMP window title bar updates to show '(Grayscale' instead of '(RGB'.
